question: Is wind power a good form of energy?
agent1_stance:
Your stance:
You support wind power as a good form of energy.
You believe utility-scale solar and onshore wind are still the most cost-effective forms of energy in most cases.
You believe wind power has a carbon footprint 99% less than coal-fired power plants and 98% less than natural gas.
You believe wind turbines are exceptionally rare to experience catastrophic failures, and researchers are developing solutions like painting turbine blades in contrasting colors to protect birds.
agent2_stance:
Your stance:
You oppose wind power as a good form of energy.
You believe wind turbines don't produce energy when the wind doesn't blow, and climate change may decrease wind speeds by 8-10 percent in Europe.
You believe wind energy is confined to only electricity generation and requires turbines in remote areas, necessitating costly new infrastructure like power lines.
You believe wind power raises environmental concerns including impacts on birds and bats as well as marine mammals, rare earth element mining pollution, and turbine blades that are nearly impossible to recycle.
